title: inbox: keyId without #fragment causes actor mismatch rejection
description:
src/server.rs, handle_inbox, lines 805-923

The code derives actor_uri from sig_parts.key_id by splitting on '#' and taking the first segment:

    let actor_uri = sig_parts.key_id.split('#').next().unwrap_or(&sig_parts.key_id).to_string();

This assumes keyId is of the form https://actor-url#key-fragment (the Mastodon default). However, some ActivityPub implementations (e.g. PeerTube, some Pleroma/Akkoma configs) use a key URL that is a distinct URL path with no fragment, such as https://example.com/users/bob/keys/main.

In that case, split('#').next() returns the full keyId URL. The actor_uri derived is https://example.com/users/bob/keys/main, but activity["actor"] is https://example.com/users/bob. The mismatch check at line 916 fires and returns 401 UNAUTHORIZED, silently dropping all follows and Undo activities from these servers.

Fix: after the split-on-# derivation, also try fetching the keyId URL and extracting its owner field as a fallback actor_uri (or accept that actor_uri could be either the actor or a separate key document, and validate against the actor_cache owner field).
close_reason: Fixed: keyId without # used as-is for actor URI
